Flat head syndrome (plagiocephaly and brachycephaly) 
Some babies get a flat head when they’re a few months old because they spend a lot of time lying down. It’s usually nothing to worry about and gets better as they get older.
Symptoms of flat head syndrome 
The main symptom of flat head syndrome is a flattened head.
If your baby has flat head syndrome, their head may be:
flat on 1 side – it may look uneven and their ears may not line up (plagiocephaly) flat at the back – it may look wide and the front of their head may bulge outwards (brachycephaly) Apart from affecting how your baby’s head looks, flat head syndrome does not cause any other symptoms. It does not affect their brain or development.
Credit: Multimedia Team - nhs.uk
Non-urgent advice: See a GP if:
you're concerned about the shape or size of your baby’s head your baby’s head is always tilted to one side, or they have difficulty turning their head 
How to avoid flat head syndrome or stop it getting worse 
There’s no need to worry if your baby has flat head syndrome because it will usually correct itself.
But there are things you can do to prevent them getting a flat head or
